rulings.Case Title: BLAYLOCK ROBERT VAN, LLC VS. ROBERT NEIL CHEATHAM
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Hearing On Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award Pursuant To Ccp Sections 1285 Et Seq. And For Entry Of Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, May 5, 2014, Line 4: PETITIONER BLAYLOCK ROBERT VAN, LLC'S Hearing On Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award Pursuant To CCP Sections 1285 Et Seq. And For Entry Of Judgment. Off calendar. Petitioner has not demonstrated proper service of the petition. (CCP § 1290.4.) On March 25, 2014, Petitioner served the petition and other documents by first class mail, certified mail, return receipt requested. However, Petitioner did not include a copy of the arbitration agreement with his Petition to Confirm Contractual Arbitration. There is no evidence that Petitioner "served in the manner provided in the arbitration agreement for the service of such petition and notice." (CCP § 1290.4(a).) "Service within this State shall be made in the manner provided by law for the service of summons in an action." (CCP § 1290.4(b)(1).) Service by first class mail, certified mail, return receipt requested is insufficient. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: IN THE MATTER OF THE PETITION OF HIRECOUNSEL LOS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Verified Petition For Order For Pre-Litigation Discovery
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, May 05, 2014, Line 2, PETITIONER IN THE MATTER OF THE PETITION OF HIRECOUNSEL LOS ANGELES' Verified Petition For Order For Pre-Litigation Discovery Pro Tem Judge William Shapiro, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: FATIMA GUILLEN VS. UNIVISION TELEVISION GROUP, INC. A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, May 5, 2014, Line 11: PLAINTIFF FATIMA GUILLEN'S Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery. Hearing required. Plaintiff's motion to compel discovery from Defendant Univision Television Group, Inc. is granted in part and denied in part with a hearing required on the adequacy and terms of the "opt-out" notice and procedure to be approved by the Court. The motion is granted insofar as the wage statements must be produced without redaction of the contact information unless an individual employee has opted out of providing contact information in accordance with Pioneer Electronics (USA), Inc., v. Superior Court (2007) 40 Cal.4th 360, 372 - 373 and Belaire-West v. Superior Court (2007) 149 Cal.App.4th 554, 557-558. Where the employee has opted-out, the contact information should be redacted. All wage statements should be marked "Attorney Eyes Only" and treated accordingly under the terms of the protective order. The motion is denied to the extent that the parties did not implement an approved opt-out notice procedure to protect the employees' privacy interests prior to propounding the request for production of employment records. Prior to the production of the records, an opt-out notice must be sent to the employees by an independent claims administrator, the terms of the notice and notice procedure to be approved by the Court. Monetary sanctions of $ 200.00 are awarded in favor of Plaintiff Fatima Guillen and against Defendant Univision Television Group, Inc. to be paid within 30 days of service of notice of entry of order . The grounds for the majority of objections to the requests were not substantially justified nor did defendants timely seek the protective orders required to protect third party privacy interests. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: FATIMA GUILLEN VS. UNIVISION TELEVISION GROUP, INC. A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Deft To Respond To Special Interrogatories, Set 1
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, May 5, 2014, Line 11: PLAINTIFF FATIMA GUILLEN'S Motion To Compel Defendant To Respond To Special Interrogatories, Set 1. Denied. Assuming that Univision Management, Co. produces complete documents pursuant to the ruling pursuant to the ruling on Plaintiff's Motion to Compel Responses to Discovery, a designation pursuant to CCP § 2030.230 is a sufficient response to the interrogatories. The document request only seeks a single well-defined category of documents, no further designation of the documents is required under CCP § 2030.230 in the course of responding to the interrogatory. Monetary sanctions are denied. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: JEFFREY A. JOHNSON VS. ASHFIELD CAPITAL PARTNERS LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, May 5, 2014, Line 12: DEFENDANTS ASHFIELD CAPITAL PARTNERS LLC, OLD MUTUAL HOLDINGS INC., and OLD MUTUAL ASSET MANAGEMENT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication. Granted as to Defendants Old Mutual Holdings, Inc. and Old Mutual Asset Management's (collectively "Old Mutual") motion for summary judgment and denied as to Ashfield Capital Partners, LLC's ("Ashfield") motion for summary judgment. Granted as to the motion for summary adjudication Issue nos. 3 and 6, and denied as to Issue nos. 1, 2, 4, 5. Old Mutual has met its initial burden of proof to show that as the parent company of Ashfield, Old Mutual did not employ Plaintiff or was responsible for Plaintiff's termination. Plaintiff has not met his shifting burden. Accordingly, Issue no. 1 is moot in light of the granting of Old Mutual's motion for summary judgment. For Issue no. 2, Ashfield has not met its initial burden of proof to show that it did not retaliate against Plaintiff by failing to consider Plaintiff for other open positions prior to or after termination. (See Morgan v. Regents of University of Cal. (2000) 88 Cal.App.4th 52, 67.) Further, by proffering new evidence in reply to rebut Plaintiff's opposition evidence, Defendants have further demonstrated that there are triable issues of fact on the issue of retaliation. For Issue nos. 4 and 5, Ashfield's argument is predicated on the granting of summary adjudication against Plaintiff's retaliation and misclassification claims. Since there are triable issues of fact regarding Plaintiff's retaliation claims, summary adjudication is denied. For Issue no. 3, Defendant has met its initial burden to demonstrate that Plaintiff was properly classified as an exempt employee. In turn, Plaintiff has not met his shifting burden. For Issue no. 6, Ashfield has met its initial burden to demonstrate that Plaintiff cannot establish his defamation claim. In turn, Plaintiff has not met his shifting burden. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN TURNER VS. UNIVERSAL MANUFACTURING CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Ntc
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, May 5, 2014, Line 15: DEFENDANT UNIVERSAL MANUFACTURING CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion is denied because defendant failed to maintain its initial burden of production. The "completed and accepted doctrine" applies where the defect is patent. See Sanchez v. Swinerton & Walberg (1996) 47 Cal.App.4th 1461, 1470. In this case, defendant presents no evidence regarding the step, what the defect was, and whether the defect was patent or latent. In addition, Ex. D to the declaration of David Hart creates a triable issue of material fact regarding whether defendant retained control over the tower and/or whether American Bridge accepted defendant's work. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order that complies with CCP 437c(g) with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/AJR)
